cards.front: How do you form the past tense when the stem's last vowel is ㅏ or ㅗ?
cards.grammarNote: Stems with last vowel ㅏ or ㅗ take 았어요: 가다 → 갔어요, 오다 → 왔어요. With these vowels the stem and 아 contract: 가+았 = 갔.

cards.front: How do you form the past tense for all other vowel stems?
cards.grammarNote: All stems not ending in ㅏ/ㅗ take 었어요: 먹다 → 먹었어요, 마시다 → 마셨어요. 하다 verbs are the special case: 하다 → 했어요.

cards.front: I did / I studied (공부하다 — past tense)
cards.grammarNote: All 하다 verbs become 했어요 in the past. This is one of the most useful rules — it covers thousands of Korean verbs built with 하다.

cards.front: There was / I had (있다 — past tense)
I did not eat (negation with 안)
cards.grammarNote: Place 안 directly before the verb to negate past actions: 안 + past verb. This is the standard 'did not' construction.

cards.front: I could not go (negation with 못)
